Package batch

Provides the core orchestration logic for batch-processing media metadata.

See: Description

  • Class Summary 
    Class Description
    BatchBuilder
    Implements the Builder design pattern for constructing and validating BatchConfiguration instances used by batch-processing operations.
    BatchConfiguration
    An immutable configuration object representing the validated parameters for a batch processing operation.
    BatchMetadataUtils  
    DisplayMetadata
    Utility class to print media metadata in a format emulating the output style of exiftool -G1 -a -s -u.
    MediaBatchProcessor
    Automates the batch processing of media files by copying, renaming, and chronologically sorting them according to metadata timestamps.
    MediaMetadataConsole
    The primary Command Line Interface (CLI) entry point for media metadata operations.
    MediaRecord
    Represents an immutable snapshot of a media file's properties read during a scan.
    MetadataScanner
    Facilitates the discovery and metadata extraction of media files within a directory tree.
  • Exception Summary 
    Exception Description
    BatchErrorException
    This exception is thrown when the running application is terminated abruptly.

Package batch Description

Provides the core orchestration logic for batch-processing media metadata.

The package is designed around a "Surgical" processing model, which ensures that source media is never modified in place. Instead, it facilitates a workflow of discovery, configuration, and execution:

Primary Components:

  • MediaMetadataConsole: The CLI entry point that parses user arguments and initializes the application lifecycle.
  • BatchBuilder: A fluent API used to construct an immutable BatchConfiguration.
  • MetadataScanner: Handles the recursive discovery of media files and extracts initial metadata to create a sorted set of records.
  • MediaBatchProcessor: The execution engine that performs the physical file copying, renaming, and binary metadata patching.

Data Flow:

  1. The user provides input via the Console.
  2. A BatchConfiguration is built to define the rules (prefix, date overrides, etc.).
  3. The MetadataScanner crawls the source and generates MediaRecord entries.
  4. The MediaBatchProcessor consumes these records to produce the final organised output.
Since:
5 May 2026
Version:
1.1
Author:
Trevor Maggs
